bytes.topic: Dev Tooling — Stylelint, Prettier for CSS, PostCSS pipeline
bytes.title: Sass + Stylelint + PostCSS — A Lint Pipeline That Actually Understands Your Partials
bytes.body:
Stylelint's default config chokes on Sass-specific syntax like `@use`, `@forward`, and `%placeholders`. The fix: swap the standard parser for `postcss-scss` and extend `stylelint-config-standard-scss` instead of `stylelint-config-standard`.

In your `.stylelintrc.json`, set `"customSyntax": "postcss-scss"` so Stylelint parses interpolations (`#{$var}`), `@each`, and `@mixin` without false positives. Pair this with `stylelint-scss` for Sass-aware rules — `scss/no-duplicate-dollar-variables`, `scss/at-rule-no-unknown`, and `scss/dollar-variable-pattern` are worth enabling from day one.

For Prettier, set `"parser": "scss"` in your config or rely on file-extension detection. Prettier 3+ handles nested rules and Sass control flow well, but it won't touch `@extend` chains — keep those short manually.

The PostCSS pipeline slot matters: run `postcss-import` first to resolve `@use` aliases, then Autoprefixer, then cssnano in production. Don't run cssnano during lint — it mutates selectors in ways that break Stylelint's source-map tracking. Wire everything together in `postcss.config.mjs` with environment guards.
